headline: Tech tries to rebound after AI/chip sell-off
detail: The sector-wide AI sell-off is the direct cause of pain across your book — PLTR, AVGO, and CRWD are all down double digits. A one-day bounce doesn't mean the selling is over, especially with macro headwinds still present. On margin, you need sustained follow-through, not just a dead-cat bounce, before relaxing.

headline: Micron surges 9% on memory revenue boom
detail: Micron's blowout quarter signals real AI infrastructure demand — memory prices are spiking as the compute buildout accelerates. This is a tailwind for chip names broadly, including AVGO and SMH, and supports the thesis behind your semiconductor exposure. Watch if SMH can hold and build on this; it's your one chip name currently in the green.

headline: Hidden inflation triggers could force a Fed rate hike
detail: Beyond oil, structural inflation pressures are building — this raises the real possibility of a rate hike rather than cuts, which is toxic for high-multiple tech names like PLTR and CRWD. On margin, rising rates also increase your borrowing cost directly. This is the single biggest macro risk to your entire concentrated book right now.

headline: SpaceX 'investment coma' drives space ETF to worst month in 6 years
detail: This headline is directly relevant to your SPCX and SPAL positions — sentiment around SpaceX-linked vehicles is deteriorating sharply. SPAL is already down 14.48% and its 2x leverage means the decay accelerates in choppy, down-trending conditions. These two positions deserve active, daily attention — not set-and-forget.
